metadata.greeting: Get ready for a fun-filled weekend in London! I've found some fantastic free and engaging activities perfect for a 3-year-old, with a mix of outdoor adventures and indoor explorations. From free museum visits with hands-on exhibits to exciting Easter events, there's something for every little explorer this weekend.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for toddlers, offers a safe and engaging space to play and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford. The playground is within walking distance of the station.
transport.tip: Bring a picnic to enjoy in the park after playing.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A charming city farm with animals and an Italian cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for seeing farm animals up close and enjoying a snack at the cafe.

transport.fromHackney: Walk or take a local bus to Hackney City Farm.
transport.tip: Check for animal feeding times to make the visit extra special.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers an engaging space to play and explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford. The playground is within walking distance of the station.
transport.tip: Check for special events for babies and toddlers

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm Easter Activities
activities.description: Free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a fun and interactive Easter experience with animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria line to Vauxhall.
transport.tip: Check for specific times for Easter activities.

activities.title: Coram's Fields
activities.description: Seven acres dedicated entirely to children with an adventure playground, sand pits, a paddling pool, and a small city farm.
activities.whyItWorks: A great place for kids to let off steam with a variety of play options.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube to Russell Square and walk to Coram's Fields.
transport.tip: Perfect for a summer day with the paddling pool.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the history of homes and domestic life.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging exhibits for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube to Hoxton.
transport.tip: Check for family trails.

activities.title: Free Easter Events at Camden Market
activities.description: Easter Weekender with activities including an Easter art trail, face painting, and bonnet making.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a vibrant and interactive Easter experience with street performances.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Camden Road or the Northern Line to Camden Town.
transport.tip: Entry to the market and core Easter trail is free.

activities.title: Little Angel Theatre
activities.description: A dedicated puppet theatre with shows for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers engaging and imaginative puppet shows perfect for 3-year-olds.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus or tube to Angel station and walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as shows are popular.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, animals, and the natural world.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers engaging exhibits that are perfect for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Visit the dinosaurs and the interactive exhibits.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is an interactive space designed for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Don't miss The Garden in the basement.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Giant Lindt gold bunnies are dotted around the grounds, and once you've found them all you can get a little chocolate rabbit to take home.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun easter egg hunt with chocolate.

transport.fromHackney: Train from Waterloo to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Track down six giant Easter eggs on the zoo grounds and solve the code written on them.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun easter egg hunt with animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Ask at entry.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A popular park with a Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and surrounding beach, sensory trail, and teepees.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of play areas suitable for different ages and interests.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Notting Hill Gate or Queensway and walk to Kensington Gardens.
transport.tip: Arrive early to avoid long queues.

activities.title: V&A Museum of Childhood
activities.description: A museum specifically designed with children in mind, with plenty to keep them entertained.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive exhibits and play areas suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Avoid peak hours for a quieter experience.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: A family-friendly museum with a small farm area, hands-on exhibits, and beautiful gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of activities including animals, gardens, and interactive exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Visit the farm area and gardens on a warm day.

activities.title: Cutty Sark
activities.description: Explore a historic sailing ship and learn about maritime history.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a fun and educational experience for young children with interactive exhibits and play areas.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark.
transport.tip: Check for family activities and events.

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: A musical adaptation of the beloved Paddington Bear stories.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a fun and engaging experience for young children with music, dance, and storytelling.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Covent Garden or Charing Cross and walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Bluey at Kew Gardens
activities.price: Included with admission to Kew Gardens
activities.description: Bluey-themed interactive activities for the whole family, including a Play Zone with 3D installations and the chance to meet the Bluey character.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a fun and interactive experience with a popular children's character.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube and train to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: Disney's award-winning musical with stunning visuals and music.
activities.whyItWorks: A visually spectacular show with a great story and memorable songs.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Covent Garden or Temple and walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Follow the themed egg trail, climb and explore across indoor and outdoor play, and meet animals along the way.
activities.whyItWorks: A cracking Easter adventure at Hobbledown Heath.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Hounslow West.
transport.tip: Most families spend 4–5 hours, with many staying for the full day to explore everything the park has to offer.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Sounds of Blossom
activities.description: Experience stunning cherry blossom and magnolia displays accompanied by live music performances throughout the gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Beautiful displays and live music.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube and train to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Southbank Centre Spring Family Fun
activities.description: A variety of family-friendly events and activities throughout the Easter holidays.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a wide range of engaging activities for children of all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Waterloo, Embankment, or Southbank.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific event listings and booking information.

activities.title: Wicked
activities.description: The untold story of the Witches of Oz
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a magical and visually stunning experience for older children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Victoria Station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Ham House and Garden Easter Trail
activities.description: Family Easter egg hunt
activities.whyItWorks: Fun easter egg hunt.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube and bus to Ham House and Garden.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.
